title: identity: secret_bytes() / hpke_secret_bytes() return plain [u8;32] — key material lingers on stack
description:
secret_bytes() and hpke_secret_bytes() in core/src/identity.rs return plain [u8; 32], not Zeroizing<[u8; 32]>.

Two concrete problems:

1. Inside to_secret_bytes_64() (lines 101-102), the return values of self.secret_bytes() and self.hpke_secret_bytes() are unzeroized stack temporaries. They get copied into the Zeroizing output, but the originals on the stack are not cleared — they linger until overwritten by subsequent stack frames.

2. tui/src/main.rs line 124: let hpke_secret = identity.hpke_secret_bytes(); stores the HPKE secret in a plain [u8; 32] local variable. This is moved into AppState::new() which wraps it in Zeroizing::new() at construction (app.rs line 156), but the local in main.rs is not zeroized — it leaves a copy of the secret on the stack until that frame is overwritten.

The round-57 fix (nie-tl6f.1) addressed the return type of to_secret_bytes_64() but did not fix the methods themselves or these call sites.

DESIGN.md checklist item 1: 'Never log, print, or include in error strings' — extending this to: key material must not linger on the stack as unzeroized copies.
